title: AWS resource tags should include valid format
description: AWS resource tags should have valid formatAnalyze your codeconsistency-formattedmaintainabilityCode SmellAmazon Web Services (AWS) resources tags are metadata labels with keys and optional values used to categorize and manage resources.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoProper tagging enhances resource discovery, lifecycle management, and overall productivity within the AWS environment. If tags do not comply with the AWS format, it can lead to confusion and inefficiency in managing resources, as well as unexpected behavior of the system.AWS resource tags should comply with the format stated in AWS documentation. That is, tag keys should:Be between 1 and 128 characters longConsist of Unicode letters, digits, white spaces, and the following characters:_ .
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Resources:
  MyBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: "invalid/key"  # Non-compliant: contains / character
          Value: value1
        - Key: "another:invalid"  # Non-compliant: contains : character
          Value: value2
compliant:
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: 2010-09-09
Resources:
  MyBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      Tags:
        - Key: "valid-key"  # Compliant: uses allowed characters
          Value: value1
        - Key: "aws:environment"  # Compliant: follows AWS naming convention
          Value: production
